projectDescription: A framework for building cross-platform desktop applications using JavaScript, HTML, and CSS by embedding Chromium and Node.js.
keyFeatures: Cross-platform desktop apps (Windows, macOS, Linux) from a single JavaScript codebase
Embedded Chromium for rendering HTML/CSS/JS user interfaces
Full Node.js integration in the main process for filesystem, networking, and OS access
Inter-process communication (IPC) between main and renderer processes
Native OS integrations: menus, tray, notifications, dialogs, file system
Context isolation and sandbox for secure renderer processes
Auto-updater support for delivering app updates
Electron Forge for packaging, building, and distributing apps
publicApiSurface: app — Application lifecycle and events
BrowserWindow — Create and control browser windows
ipcMain / ipcRenderer — Inter-process communication
contextBridge — Securely expose APIs from preload scripts to renderer
session — Manage browser sessions, cookies, cache, and proxies
protocol — Register custom URI protocols
shell — Open files and URLs using native applications
dialog — Display native system dialogs
Menu / MenuItem — Create native application menus
Tray — Add system tray icons and menus
Notification — Display native desktop notifications
clipboard — Read/write to the system clipboard
nativeImage — Handle images in tray, dock, and application icons
screen — Retrieve screen size, display, and cursor info
webContents — Render and control web pages
UtilityProcess — Spawn child processes with Node.js environment
autoUpdater — Enable apps to automatically update themselves
crashReporter — Submit crash reports to remote servers
globalShortcut — Register global keyboard shortcuts
powerMonitor — Monitor power state changes
